items.question: Do we need to be officially married to book a "honeymoon" package?
items.answer: No — most resorts don't check, though a few request proof for specific perks. We'll flag if that applies to a resort you're considering.

items.question: Is a private dinner really private?
items.answer: Usually yes — typically set up on a quiet stretch of beach or a private deck, though the exact setup varies by resort. We'll confirm specifics before booking.

items.question: Can we combine a resort honeymoon with a local island stay?
items.answer: Yes — several couples do a few nights on a quieter local island first, then finish at the honeymoon resort. See our Combo page for how that's structured.

items.question: What's the best time of year for a quiet, adults-only resort?
items.answer: Shoulder season (roughly April–May or October–November) tends to be quieter with lower rates than peak December–March. See our Best Time to Visit guide for more.
